description: Pre-merge audit of 28 user stories, 9,200 lines across 60 files. Verifies implementation correctness, test integrity, and architectural soundness before merging to main.

summary.verdict: DO NOT MERGE — 4 critical findings must be resolved first

findings.title: progressToken mismatch in backgroundIndex.ts
findings.description: backgroundIndex.ts computes the progress token using `Date.now()` in two separate statements (lines 81 and 83). The token sent in the workDoneProgress/create request and the token stored for subsequent progress/report calls are different values. If even 1ms elapses between the two Date.now() calls, progress reports reference a token the client never accepted. Progress reporting is silently broken.
findings.fix: Compute the token string once into a const, then use that const in both the create call and the stored progressToken field.
findings.test_gap: backgroundIndex.test.ts does not test background indexing at all — it tests workspaceSymbol instead. The test admits the server doesn't have a real workspace root.

findings.title: Cancellation tests do not test cancellation
findings.description: All 5 tests in cancellation.test.ts send normal (non-cancelled) requests and verify the response is defined. The cancel notification in test 1 uses a Date.now() requestId that doesn't match the actual JSON-RPC auto-assigned request ID. The cancellation is never actually exercised. All 5 tests would pass identically if every CancellationToken check were removed from all handlers.
findings.evidence: cancellation.test.ts: cancel notification uses fabricated request ID; tests only check `result != null`
findings.fix: Rewrite cancellation tests using raw JSON-RPC (pattern from completion.test.ts lines 427-509): send request, capture its ID, send $/cancelRequest with that same ID, verify empty/null response.
findings.test_gap: US-024 'passes: true' but the tests do not verify the feature works.

findings.title: Background indexing tests do not test background indexing
findings.description: backgroundIndex.test.ts test 1 opens a file and checks workspace/symbol finds it — this tests workspaceSymbol, not background indexing. Test 3 checks concurrent requests respond with defined results, which is trivially true. Neither test verifies that workspace files are discovered and indexed on startup.
findings.evidence: backgroundIndex.test.ts test comments admit the server doesn't have a real workspace root
findings.fix: Either (a) create a test server with workspaceRoot pointing to a temp directory containing .pike files, or (b) test indexWorkspaceFiles/discoverFiles functions directly by providing a real directory and verifying the workspace index is populated.

findings.title: removeUnusedVariableLine produces invalid edit on single-line files
findings.description: codeAction.ts removeUnusedVariableLine computes `line - 1` for the start position when deleting a line. For a single-line file (line 0), this produces start.line = -1, which is an invalid LSP Position. The optional chaining (`lines[line - 1]?.length`) prevents a crash but the resulting TextEdit has a negative line number.
findings.fix: Add guard: if line === 0, the start position should be { line: 0, character: 0 } (start of file).

findings.title: Real-codebase verification only tests crash resistance, not correctness
findings.description: US-026 acceptance criteria require testing against Pike's 555-file stdlib with 14+ representative files, zero wrong answers (P1), documented P2 findings, and measured semantic token coverage. The actual tests use ~30 corpus files and only verify `expect(result).toBeDefined()`. They cannot detect wrong answers. No P2 findings documented. No token coverage measured.
findings.fix: Add correctness spot-checks for at least 3 representative files: verify documentSymbol returns expected symbol names, definition resolves to expected locations, hover contains expected type signatures.
findings.note: US-026 should be marked 'passes: false' until corrected.

findings.title: Version bump reverted, release criteria not met
findings.description: Version was explicitly reverted from 1.0.0 to 0.1.0-alpha per user request. This is intentional. However, the remaining US-028 criteria (VSIX build, standalone build, GitHub release) are also not met. The story should be marked 'passes: false' or decomposed into a post-merge task.
findings.fix: Mark US-028 as 'passes: false'. Create a separate post-merge task for the actual release process.

findings.title: Add-missing-import only matches stdlib modules, not workspace modules
findings.description: US-019 acceptance criterion requires matching workspace modules as well as stdlib modules. codeAction.ts:154 only checks ctx.stdlibModules. No workspace module matching is implemented.
findings.fix: Add workspace module lookup via WorkspaceIndex.getAllEntries() or a pre-built module name set.

findings.title: Cache invalidation does not check Pike version
findings.description: US-022 acceptance criterion requires cache invalidation when Pike version changes. persistentCache.ts only checks WASM hash, not `pike --version`. If Pike is upgraded without tree-sitter-pike changing, the cache will be stale.
findings.evidence: persistentCache.ts: loadCache validates wasmHash but not pike version
findings.fix: Add pike version to cache metadata. Compute via `pike --version` at startup and compare with cached value.

findings.title: known-limitations.md has stale cross-file inheritance entry
findings.description: known-limitations.md states 'wireInheritance() only resolves same-file inheritance -- cross-file inherited scopes remain empty in the symbol table'. This is now false — wireCrossFileInheritance() explicitly handles cross-file inheritance.
findings.evidence: symbolTable.ts:1754-1870 wireCrossFileInheritance creates synthetic scopes for cross-file classes
findings.fix: Remove or update the stale entry in known-limitations.md.

findings.title: Decision 0022 says 'Bun Glob class' but implementation uses readdir
findings.description: docs/decisions/0022-background-indexing.md says 'Use Bun Glob class' for file discovery. The actual implementation (backgroundIndex.ts discoverFiles) uses node:fs/promises readdir with a manual recursive walk.
findings.evidence: backgroundIndex.ts: discoverFiles uses readdir; decision 0022 says 'Bun Glob'
findings.fix: Update decision 0022 to match implementation: 'recursive directory walk with node:fs/promises readdir'.

findings.title: No incremental semantic token support
findings.description: US-014 acceptance criterion requires 'Incremental token updates on didChange (delta encoding per LSP spec)'. Only full token requests are supported. The capability registers full: true but no delta support.
findings.evidence: server.ts:308-311 semanticTokensProvider: { legend, full: true } — no delta property
findings.fix: Remove the acceptance criterion or implement delta support. For v0.1.0-alpha, documenting this as a known limitation is acceptable.

findings.title: other-editors.md not verified
findings.description: US-025 acceptance criterion requires 'docs/other-editors.md verified current for Neovim and generic LSP clients'. No changes were made to this file.
findings.evidence: No diff for other-editors.md in the branch
findings.fix: Read other-editors.md, verify Neovim and generic LCP instructions are current with the new capabilities, update if needed.

findings.title: No CI workflow for benchmarks
findings.description: US-027 acceptance criterion requires 'CI workflow added that runs benchmarks'. No .github/workflows/benchmark.yml or equivalent exists.
findings.evidence: No workflow file in the branch diff
findings.fix: Add benchmark workflow or remove the criterion.

findings.title: Signature help only resolves predef.* stdlib functions
findings.description: signatureHelp.ts resolveSignature only looks up `predef.${calleeName}` in the stdlib index. Module-qualified functions (e.g., Stdio.read_file) are not resolved. Cross-file workspace functions are not resolved.
findings.fix: For v1, document as known limitation. For future: resolve module-qualified names via workspace index.

findings.title: Workspace symbol search has no result limit
findings.description: workspaceSymbol.ts returns all matching results with no upper bound. A workspace with thousands of files matching a short prefix could produce very large responses.
findings.evidence: workspaceSymbol.ts:57-103 collects all matches without limit
findings.fix: Add a configurable maxResults limit (e.g., 100). Return partial results with an indicator if truncated.

findings.title: Type resolution fallback ternary duplicated in 3 files
findings.description: The pattern `declaredType && !PRIMITIVE_TYPES.has(declaredType) ? declaredType : assignedType` appears in typeResolver.ts, completion.ts, and server.ts. If the fallback logic changes, all three must be updated in lockstep.
findings.evidence: typeResolver.ts:98-101, completion.ts (similar pattern), server.ts hover handler
findings.fix: Extract a shared helper (e.g., getEffectiveTypeName(decl: Declaration) in symbolTable.ts) and use it in all three consumers.

findings.title: persistentCache.saveCache JSDoc says 'never throws' but it does throw
findings.description: persistentCache.ts line 113 comment says 'Errors are caught and logged -- never throws' but the function throws new Error() on write failure. The caller (server.ts onShutdown) wraps in try/catch, so no runtime bug, but the API contract is misleading.
findings.evidence: persistentCache.ts line 113: throws on write failure despite JSDoc saying otherwise
findings.fix: Either make the function truly non-throwing (catch and log) or update the JSDoc to document the throw.

findings.title: pikeBinaryPath not wired through didChangeConfiguration
findings.description: server.ts reads initOpts?.pikeBinaryPath during onInitialize but onDidChangeConfiguration only wires diagnosticMode, diagnosticDebounceMs, and maxNumberOfProblems. Users changing the Pike binary path in settings must restart the server.
findings.fix: Wire pike.languageServer.path through onDidChangeConfiguration to worker.updateConfig(). One-line fix.

findings.title: Semantic token LSP tests use rough lower bounds
findings.description: semanticTokens.test.ts protocol tests check `result.data.length >= 15` or `>= 20`. These rough bounds could pass with incorrect tokens (e.g., all tokens being 'variable' type). The unit tests are more precise.
findings.fix: Decode the delta-encoded token array and assert specific token types at specific positions for at least one test case.

findings.title: definition.test.ts has conditional assertions that silently skip
findings.description: Lines 419, 444-450, 456-462, 755-762 wrap assertions in `if (variable)` blocks. If tree-sitter fails to resolve the variable, the assertion is silently skipped rather than failing.
findings.fix: Add `else { fail('expected to find ...') }` to make skipped assertions explicit failures.

findings.title: completion.test.ts has conditional sortText assertion
findings.description: Line 287-289: the sortText comparison is inside `if (localWrite && predefWrite)` — if tree-sitter doesn't resolve both, the key assertion is silently skipped.
findings.fix: Add else clause with explicit fail.

findings.title: persistentCache uses synchronous require('node:fs').readFileSync in ESM module
findings.description: computeWasmHash uses require('node:fs').readFileSync to read the WASM file. This blocks the event loop during startup/shutdown for a ~1MB file. The rest of the module uses async fs.
findings.fix: Convert to async fs.readFile. The callers (server.ts startup/shutdown) are already async.

findings.title: computeWasmHash comment says 'simple marker' but actually hashes the WASM file
findings.fix: Update comment to describe what the code actually does.

findings.title: No dedicated scope-leak tests for while/switch/do-while
findings.description: US-005 acceptance criterion requires 'Add 3+ tests: one per construct, verifying variables don't leak'. No dedicated test file or test case was found that explicitly verifies scope isolation for while, switch, or do-while blocks. Correctness is implicitly tested through the broader suite.
findings.evidence: No test file explicitly testing while/switch/do-while scope isolation found in diff
findings.fix: Add 3 explicit tests: declare a variable inside a while/switch/do-while block, verify it's not visible outside.

findings.title: CHANGELOG.md has duplicate headers
findings.description: CHANGELOG.md has duplicate ## Phase 5 AutoDoc Redesign headers and duplicate ### Fixed / ### Added subsections, suggesting mechanical merging without cleanup.
findings.fix: Deduplicate CHANGELOG headers.

stories_assessment.note: Code correct but tests are fake (F-002)
other-editors.md not verified (F-012), stale docs (F-009, F-010)
Tests only check crashes, not correctness (F-005)
Version reverted, release criteria not met (F-006)

architecture_assessment.dependency_graph: Clean DAG — no circular dependencies. Leaf features depend only on types from symbolTable. server.ts is composition root.
architecture_assessment.layering: Sound. Each feature module has clear single responsibility. Consistent handler-registration pattern.
architecture_assessment.god_objects: symbolTable.ts (1882 lines) is a near-god module housing types, builder, inheritance wiring, query API, and type inference helpers.

required_fixes_before_merge: F-001: Fix progressToken mismatch in backgroundIndex.ts
F-004: Fix single-line file edge case in codeAction.ts
F-002: Rewrite cancellation tests to actually test cancellation
F-003: Rewrite background index tests to actually test background indexing
F-009: Remove stale cross-file inheritance entry from known-limitations.md
F-010: Fix decision 0022 to match implementation
recommended_fixes_before_merge: F-005: Add correctness spot-checks to real-codebase-verification.test.ts
F-007: Add workspace module matching to add-missing-import code action
F-017: Fix persistentCache.saveCache JSDoc or behavior
deferred_to_post_merge: F-019-F-025: Low-severity test improvements and cleanup
